rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by tracing invalid identifiers or helper declarations and resolving root causes.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or data types not present in the analyzed model.
Move implementation-level bindings from component type annexes to implementation annexes when needed.
generic_rules: Validate that all AGREE expressions reference only visible identifiers or declared helpers.
Ensure AGREE assumptions and guarantees align with the correct AADL scope (type vs. implementation).
Avoid unsafe type conversions, especially Boolean to numeric comparisons.
Remove invalid or unsafe clauses rather than attempting to patch them with architecture changes.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type annexes are for assumptions/guarantees over visible ports; implementation annexes for subcomponent bindings.
anti_patterns: Do not add architecture elements to fix AGREE diagnostics unless they are already present in the model.
Do not attempt to resolve missing identifiers by introducing new components or ports.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about property associations
Model fusion and owner/scope preservation rules
